user_query:
I'm adding a mentoring-focused session to my graduate workshop based on my own experience working with my mentor, and I need the curriculum generator updated plus the related communications prepared.

Please do the following using the provided local files, and save all outputs under the paths listed below.

1) Update the curriculum generator (modify code and configuration):
- Input config: config/curriculum.json
- Input script: scripts/syllabus_builder.py
- Add a new session object with id 3 to config/curriculum.json with exactly the following fields and values (keep existing sessions unchanged):
  {
    "id": 3,
    "title": "Working with Mentors: Practices and Boundaries",
    "description": "Concrete strategies for effective, ethical mentor-mentee relationships, with emphasis on agenda-setting, feedback cycles, and boundary-setting.",
    "readings": [
      {"title": "Designing Your Mentoring Relationship", "author": "Kerry Ann Rockquemore", "year": 2013},
      {"title": "Mentoring in Academia: Boundaries and Best Practices", "author": "C. Lee", "year": 2018}
    ],
    "mentor_insight": "Trust is built through small, consistent commitments: agree on a cadence, send agendas beforehand, and summarize decisions by email.",
    "career_tasks": [
      "Draft a one-page mentoring compact",
      "Create a reusable 30-minute meeting agenda template",
      "Write a post-meeting summary email within 24 hours"
    ]
  }
- Modify scripts/syllabus_builder.py so that, for any session that includes these new fields, the generated syllabus includes:
  - A line beginning with "Mentor Insight:" followed by the exact mentor_insight text above.
  - A "Career Tasks:" section with bullet points for each item in career_tasks in the order given above.
- Preserve existing behavior for sessions without mentor_insight or career_tasks.
- Generate the updated syllabus:
  - Output: outputs/syllabus.md
  - The file must include the new session (id 3) with its readings, and contain the exact strings "Mentor Insight: Trust is built through small, consistent commitments: agree on a cadence, send agendas beforehand, and summarize decisions by email." and the bullet points listed under career_tasks.

2) Prepare meeting notes with action items (save as outputs/meeting_notes.md):
Include the following labeled sections and content:
- Objectives: must include the phrases "Normalize proactive mentor-mentee communication" and "Equip students with a meeting template".
- Agenda: must include items that mention "Review learning objectives", "Walk through session flow", and "Approve resource requests".
- Action Items: must include bullet points with exactly these four items:
  - Finalize slides for mentor practices
  - Print 25 handouts
  - Book breakout room
  - Collect post-session feedback via 3-question form
- Risks/Dependencies: must include the phrases "Student availability around midterms" and "Printing lead time".
Also mention that the new session titled "Working with Mentors: Practices and Boundaries" will be piloted in Week 5.

3) Draft two email messages (plain text) and save them under outputs/emails/:
- outputs/emails/students_announcement.txt
  - Subject line must be: Subject: New workshop session — Working with Mentors (pilot in Week 5)
  - Body must:
    - Announce the new session title exactly.
    - Include the exact mentor insight quote from the new session.
    - Include a short pre-work section listing the two readings by title only:
      - Designing Your Mentoring Relationship
      - Mentoring in Academia: Boundaries and Best Practices
    - Ask students to bring "one question you want to ask your mentor".
- outputs/emails/chair_approval.txt
  - Subject line must be: Subject: Request to pilot 'Working with Mentors' session (Week 5)
  - Body must:
    - Briefly justify the session by referencing "student demand for mentorship guidance".
    - Request resources: "25 printed handouts and one breakout room".
    - Include the phrase "approval requested by end of week".
    - Note that the updated syllabus has been regenerated at outputs/syllabus.md.

Constraints and notes:
- Do not remove or rename existing files; extend them as required.
- Use only the local files provided; do not add external dependencies.
- All three operations are required: update code/config to render the new fields in outputs/syllabus.md; prepare outputs/meeting_notes.md; and draft the two email files under outputs/emails/.
- Keep the content of existing sessions intact while adding the new session and new rendering behavior. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
